summary: The Netherlands runs the central-government programme Quantumveilige Cryptografie (QvC), with the intelligence service AIVD, together with CWI and TNO, publishing the PQC Migration Handbook now in its revised and extended second edition, and NCSC-NL providing guidance for organisations starting their migration. The Ministry of the Interior and Kingdom Relations has informed Parliament of the renewed handbook and the PQChoiceAssistant tool, and the central government has shown an operational quantum network deploying post-quantum cryptography alongside quantum key distribution. Dutch guidance follows the three post-quantum standards published by NIST, and the NCSC-NL TLS security guidelines recommend hybrid quantum-safe key exchange that combines classical elliptic-curve methods with ML-KEM, advising organisations by their quantum-risk profile on when to migrate.
standardFamilies: NIST post-quantum cryptography standards (three standards, named generically in NCSC-NL guidance)
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated (EU coordinated roadmap)
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete (EU coordinated roadmap)
govActors:
AIVD | General Intelligence and Security Service, co-author with CWI and TNO of the PQC Migration Handbook and statements on the transition to post-quantum cryptography
NCSC-NL | National Cyber Security Centre, explainers, migration guidance and the TLS security guidelines naming hybrid quantum-safe key exchange with ML-KEM
BZK | Ministry of the Interior and Kingdom Relations, letters and decision memoranda to Parliament on the renewed quantum-safe cryptography handbook and the PQChoiceAssistant tool
Rijksoverheid | Dutch central government, the Quantumveilige Cryptografie Rijk programme, the operational quantum network and the PQC symposium addressed by State Secretary Van Marum
Tweede Kamer | House of Representatives, answers to parliamentary questions on quantum-proof encryption and documents on the EU quantum strategy
mainRegulation:
NCSC-NL Security guidelines for Transport Layer Security (TLS) version 2025-05 | national | guidance
PQC Migration Handbook, revised and extended second edition (AIVD, CWI, TNO, 2024) | national | guidance
provenance: summary + govActors (AIVD, NCSC-NL, BZK, Rijksoverheid, Tweede Kamer): PQC Migration Handbook 2nd ed row, NCSC-NL guidance/explainer rows, BZK letters and decision memoranda rows, Rijksoverheid programme/news/symposium rows, Tweede Kamer parliamentary-question and EU-strategy rows

algorithms ML-KEM + hybridDeployment Recommended: NCSC-NL Security guidelines for TLS v2025-05 (26 June 2025) row, which names hybrid quantum-safe key exchange X25519MLKEM768, SecP256r1MLKEM768, SecP384r1MLKEM1024 combining ECDHE with ML-KEM and advises by quantum-risk profile

standardFamilies: NCSC-NL expert blog on NIST's publication of three post-quantum cryptography standards (13 August 2024) row; named only generically because FIPS 203/204/205 are not stated in the included summaries

dominantProcess EU: carried forward unchanged (EU coordination posture / taker of the EU roadmap); no included document unambiguously states a new standards alignment despite the NIST algorithm baseline

mainRegulation: only the attested national guidance instruments retained (NCSC-NL TLS guidelines v2025-05 row, PQC Migration Handbook 2nd ed row). NIS2 and DORA removed: no included Netherlands document in this corpus attests them as binding instruments; the only NIS2/PQC fiche is included:false
legalStatus soft-only: no binding instrument attested by an included Netherlands document; guidance and the QvC-Rijk programme set the recommended approach

migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: EU coordinated roadmap (NIS Cooperation Group, high-risk by 2030, full by 2035) carried forward; no national dated milestone or national end date is attested (AIVD statements warn migration may not finish in time but give no fixed date)
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2; national guidance (NCSC TLS guidelines, the PQC Migration Handbook) sets the recommended approach and the government-wide QvC-Rijk programme coordinates public-sector migration.
classificationBasis:
summary + govActors (AIVD, NCSC-NL, BZK, Rijksoverheid): PQC Migration Handbook, NCSC-NL guidance rows, BZK letter to Parliament, and Rijksoverheid programme rows
dominantProcess NIST: NCSC-NL expert blog on NIST's publication of three post-quantum cryptography standards (13 August 2024) row. No Dutch sovereign scheme stated, so NIST assigned
Tweede Kamer role: parliamentary questions and EU quantum strategy document rows
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED. The NCSC-NL row attests that NIST released three standards but the included summaries do not name FIPS 203/204/205 or ML-KEM/ML-DSA/SLH-DSA explicitly - REVIEW handbook for named algorithms
migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: OMITTED, no dated migration milestones or end date stated in the included summaries (AIVD statements warn migration may not finish in time but give no fixed date)
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
